name: Leeders Law - Bankruptcy lawyer - Chicago
description_short: Chicago-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings with 25+ years experience. Offers free consultations and multi-location service throughout Chicagoland.
description_long:
Leeders Law is an established law firm based in Chicago with over 25 years of legal experience serving individuals and small business owners throughout the Chicago metropolitan area. The firm maintains seven office locations across Chicagoland (Portage Park, downtown Chicago, O'Hare, Hyde Park, Schaumburg, Burr Ridge, and Orland Park) with flexible scheduling and virtual consultation options to accommodate client needs.

The firm's primary focus is consumer bankruptcy, specifically Chapter 7 (debt elimination) and Chapter 13 (debt restructuring) filings. Beyond bankruptcy, Leeders Law also provides legal services in personal injury (car accidents, slip and falls, nursing home injuries, workers compensation), family law (divorce, child support, custody), and landlord-tenant matters (lease drafting, commercial negotiations). The website emphasizes their role in helping clients stop creditor harassment, save money, and achieve financial fresh starts.

Leeders Law differentiates itself through multi-location accessibility, flexible hours, free initial consultations, and explicit commitment to one-on-one personal service. The firm advertises reasonable fees and aggressive representation tailored to individual circumstances. Their presence across multiple suburban locations suggests established client reach beyond downtown Chicago.

As with any legal service, the firm's effectiveness depends on individual case complexity and the specific attorney assigned. The website provides limited specific information about success rates, typical timelines, or fee structures beyond claiming "reasonable" rates. Prospective clients should request detailed fee disclosures during free consultations and verify attorney qualifications for their specific bankruptcy chapter.
address: 205 W Randolph St Suite 1401, Chicago, IL 60606

pros: 25+ years of legal experience in bankruptcy practice
Seven office locations across Chicago and suburbs with flexible scheduling
Free consultations and free bankruptcy evaluations for prospective clients
Offers both virtual/phone and in-person meeting options
Handles both Chapter 7 (debt elimination) and Chapter 13 (debt restructuring) filings
Multi-practice firm also handles personal injury, family law, and landlord-tenant matters
Emphasizes one-on-one personal service and client-centered approach
cons: Website provides no specific fee structure or pricing information beyond 'reasonable fees'
No published success rates, case outcomes, or typical timeline data for bankruptcy filings
Limited attorney profiles or credentials displayed on website to verify specializations
No client testimonials or case results shown to assess firm performance
Broad practice areas (7+ different legal services) may dilute bankruptcy specialization
best_for: Chicago-area consumers facing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy with preference for local, multi-office accessibility
Individuals with unsecured debt seeking debt elimination or home-preserving restructuring plans
Small business owners needing bankruptcy representation combined with other business legal services
diagnosis: Best for Chicago-area consumers seeking Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy with convenient local access and personalized service. Primary caveat: verify detailed fee structures and request specific bankruptcy outcome data during free consultation, as website lacks transparent pricing and case result information typical of specialized bankruptcy practices.
services: Chapter 13 Bankruptcy filing (debt restructuring and repayment plans)
Personal Injury representation (car accidents, slip and falls, nursing home injuries)
Lease drafting and structuring for small businesses
In-person meetings at seven Chicagoland office locations
